steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content: Apply the quadratic formula with $a=5, b=-9, c=-3$: $$x=\frac{9\pm\sqrt{81+60}}{10}=\frac{9\pm\sqrt{141}}{10}.$$

steps.title: Identify the greatest solution
steps.content: The larger root takes the $+$ sign: $\tfrac{9}{10}+\tfrac{\sqrt{141}}{10}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The final check is to match the computed result to the answer choices. The value or equation found above matches choice B, $\frac{9}{10} + \frac{\sqrt{141}}{10}$. The other choices do not satisfy the same condition from the question, so B is correct.
